name: Agentheim
observations: Preset-driven, local-first AI automation platform
Version 0.1.0, MIT license
Python 3.12+ required
Entry point: `agentheim` CLI via `interfaces.cli.cli:main`
GitHub: 0langa/agentheim
10 E2E browser tests (Playwright)
737 tests passed, 2 skipped (2026-05-14, excluding 1 flaky browser network test)
live-ai-testing.md is canonical always-up-to-date testing status doc
External test environment at agentheim-testing-enviroment/ for e2e validation with intentional bugs
M6-M9 AICtx integration finalized and committed (f034075)
Deleted fix_imports.py, docs/AICTX_INTEGRATION_PLAN.md, .env.example, 02-forbidden-behaviours.md alias, vendor tests, vendor CLI, remote OCI modules
Docs integrity sweep completed: all docs verified against current codebase
Created docs/CLI-COMMANDS.md as authoritative CLI reference
Governance scripts fixed: check-agent-instructions.py, roadmap-check.py
CI workflow fixed: python-version 3.14 -> 3.12
Test suite: 782 passed, 3 skipped, 0 failures
Commit pushed to GitHub master

name: Core Runtime
observations: Generic orchestration engine in `core/`
Intentionally ignorant of providers, workflows, tools, models
Key files: workflow_runner.py, ledger.py, policy_engine.py, model_registry.py, tool_protocol.py, capability_registry.py, events.py, artifact_store.py, context_packer.py, state_machine.py

Runtime phases: INIT → LOAD_CONFIG → PREPARE_WORKSPACE → SCAN_REPOSITORY → BUILD_CONTEXT_PACK → PLAN → EXECUTE_TASK → BASIC_VERIFY → VERIFY_TASK → FIX_LOOP → FINAL_VERIFY → FINAL_REPORT → RESUME_AVAILABLE → DONE

name: WorkflowRunner
observations: Executes DAGs in topological order
Handles parallel groups, retry logic, resumption
File: core/workflow_runner.py

name: RunLedger
observations: Append-only event log with SHA-256 hash chain verification
File: core/ledger.py

name: PolicyEngine
observations: Evaluates allow/deny/ask for every tool call
Risk levels: LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH, CRITICAL
File: core/policy_engine.py

name: ModelRegistry
observations: Capability-based model resolution
Lazy-loaded provider adapters via importlib
File: core/model_registry.py

name: ToolRegistry
observations: Mediates all tool invocations through PolicyEngine
File: core/tool_protocol.py

name: Seven Immutable Laws
observations: 1. Core Ignorance - core/ knows no concrete provider/workflow/tool names
2. Workflow Pack Autonomy - workflows define own agents/steps/prompts
3. Provider Interchangeability - lazy-loaded, never shape core architecture
4. Progressive Disclosure - presets for beginners, config for power users, APIs for devs
5. Event-Sourced Truth - append-only ledgers, no mutation, no side-channel state
6. Local-First Sovereignty - external services explicit, policy-gated, privacy-compatible
7. Safety By Default - all side effects through tool protocol, policy engine, approval workflow
Violating any law = architectural breach, blocks merge

name: Instruction Priority
observations: Priority order: user request > AGENTS.md > .github/instructions/*.md > .github/agents/*.agent.md > docs/ > skills/
Before planning/editing: must read AGENTS.md, .github/instructions/README.md, every non-empty .github/instructions/*.md
Scope rule: .github/instructions/ binding even when not repeated in main agent file

name: Forbidden Behaviors
observations: Level 4 (Constitutional): provider logic in core/, workflow logic in core/, AICtx logic in core/, skipping policy engine, mutating ledger events, sending secrets remote without approval, weakening auth/safety defaults, committing gitignored local repos like AICtx/

Level 3 (Architectural): importing concrete implementations into core/, direct tool execution outside protocol, hidden mutable global state, second registry/policy/ledger path, adding public commands without docs/tests, silently swallowing production errors

Level 2 (Boundary): changing event schemas without migration plan, changing artifact layout without updating consumers, adding workflow packs without registration/smoke coverage, adding providers without lazy-loading, adding generated files without ignore rules, touching multiple subsystems without explaining cross-boundary impact

name: Stop Conditions
observations: Stop and ask direction if: task requires violating immutable law, change conflicts with .github/instructions/*.md, requested change conflicts with higher-priority rule

name: Providers
observations: Lazy-loaded adapters in providers/
openai_v1: OpenAI + compatible APIs (Grok, Ollama, LM Studio)
azure_foundry: Azure AI Foundry / Azure OpenAI
aws_bedrock: AWS Bedrock
oci_genai: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure GenAI
azure-real profile uses gpt-4.1-mini on Azure Foundry endpoint (was gpt-5.4)
gpt-4.1-mini supports vision, tool calling, streaming per Azure docs
Agentheim provider layer supports vision input (image_url via base64 data URLs) but no streaming or model-level tool calling

name: Tools
observations: Mediated through ToolRegistry.invoke() with policy gating
Categories: browser (Playwright), filesystem, git, http, local_db, mcp, memory, network, shell
BrowserTool supports session mode + transient mode
Playwright E2E tests in tests/test_browser_e2e.py
MCPClient.disconnect() now kills entire process tree on Windows (psutil/taskkill fallback) — fixes orphaned npx child processes
MCPConnectionPool registers atexit cleanup to prevent leaks on unclean exit
BrowserTool, filesystem, git, http, shell tools all policy-gated

name: Workflows
observations: Workflow packs in workflows/: coding, research, documents, file_organization, docs_maintenance, github_maintenance, command_assistant, distributed
Each pack defines own agents, steps, prompts, policies, verification
Must not import provider implementations directly or mutate core state

name: Memory Subsystem
observations: Three-tier system in memory/
brain.py: unified orchestrator
episodic.py: timeline-based memory
semantic.py: concept graph memory
embeddings.py: vector embedding engine
bus.py: cross-process memory bus
registry.py: memory backend registry
tiers/: working memory, global memory

name: Interfaces
observations: CLI: interfaces/cli/cli.py (Typer-based)
API server: interfaces/api_server/app.py (FastAPI)
Web UI: interfaces/web_ui/
Desktop UI: interfaces/desktop_ui/ (PyQt6/tkinter)
Guided TUI: interfaces/guided_tui/

name: Testing
observations: DevTest runner: devtest/run-devtest.ps1 with modes narrow/targeted/directive/phase7/broad/full
Skipped tests: optional GUI-environment checks when desktop deps unavailable
pytest config in pyproject.toml: testpaths=[tests], pythonpath=[.]
Full suite: 737 passed, 2 skipped (2026-05-14)
1 browser test flaky due to external network (ERR_CONNECTION_RESET at example.com)
MCP tests: 25 passed including process tree kill and atexit cleanup

name: MCP Servers Config
observations: Agentheim has NO .ai-team/mcp.json — no MCP servers configured for Agentheim itself
MCP client code exists in tools/mcp/ but is not wired into workflows or CLI
~/.kimi/mcp.json belongs to Kimi CLI, not Agentheim

name: AGENTS.md
observations: Binding instructions for all agents
Must read before planning or editing
Points to .github/instructions/ as binding rules
Main agent: .github/agents/agentheim-autonomous-engineer.agent.md

name: docs/ARCHITECTURE.md
observations: System design, module layout, runtime phases, boundary rules

Describes 3 user layers: beginner (preset-driven), power-user (configurable), developer (extensible)

observations: Test and smoke-check guidance

name: Entry Points
observations: CLI: `agentheim` (Typer, interfaces/cli/cli.py)
Dev test: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\devtest\run-devtest.ps1 -Mode <mode>`
Tests: `pytest -q tests/` or `pytest tests/test_browser_e2e.py -m e2e`
Directive checks: `python scripts/check-agent-instructions.py`

observations: Python 3.12+, setuptools build
Key deps: openai, pydantic, typer, rich, pyyaml, fastapi, uvicorn, requests, beautifulsoup4, cryptography, numpy, pathspec
Dev deps: pytest, pytest-cov, playwright
Entry: agentheim = interfaces.cli.cli:main

name: AICtx Workspace
observations: AICtx source project now at C:/Users/juliu/source/repos/AICtx (separate workspace directory)
Previously was only a gitignored reference copy at agentheim/AICtx/
AICtx is co-developed with Agentheim — changes to AICtx APIs are allowed to improve integration
AICtx remote: https://github.com/0langa/AICtx.git

name: MCP Process Leak Fix
observations: Fixed in tools/mcp/client.py: disconnect() closes pipes then kills process tree on Windows
Uses psutil if available, falls back to taskkill /F /T on Windows, os.killpg on Unix
Fixed in tools/mcp/pool.py: atexit.register(self._atexit_cleanup) disconnects all clients on Python exit
Added tests: test_kill_proc_tree_ignores_dead_process, test_disconnect_closes_pipes_and_kills_tree, test_atexit_cleanup_disconnects_all
Root cause: subprocess.Popen.terminate() on Windows only kills top-level process, leaving npx child node.exe orphaned

name: Live AI Testing

Tracks provider smoke, full functionality, current results pass/fail/not-tested
